text.title: Agents Memory System — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `agents-memory-system` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Memory MUST be split into short-term + episodic + long-term tiers. Single-tier memory either overflows or under-recalls.
rule.rationale: Single-tier (just a buffer or just a vector store) trades one failure for another; three tiers each absorb a different load.

rule.statement: Short-term buffer is bounded by token count, not turn count. Default cap 8k tokens.
rule.rationale: Turn-count caps lose long messages; token caps respect the actual context window.

rule.statement: Every N turns (default 10), the short-term buffer is summarised into an episodic memory entry.
rule.rationale: Without periodic summarisation, episodic memory either doesn't exist or is too granular to be useful.

rule.statement: Long-term recall uses hybrid scoring (BM25 + vector cosine). Pure vector recall misses keyword matches; pure BM25 misses semantic.
rule.rationale: Hybrid recall ~30% more accurate than either method alone on long-tail queries.

rule.statement: Episodic memory has an explicit eviction policy (LRU or TTL). Unbounded episodic store grows linearly and slows recall.
rule.rationale: Without eviction, recall latency grows with session count; LRU/TTL keeps it bounded.
